---
title: "Functions"
---

# Functions

The package exposes a small set of user-facing functions. The main entry point
is `path_features()`, which returns the notation used in the paper.

::: {.function-list}

::: {.function-item}
::: {.function-call}
`path_features(path, depth = 4)`
:::
::: {.function-body}
Computes the low-order path coordinates for a two-column path
`cbind(u, F)`. The returned columns are `p2`, `p_pm`, `p3u`, `p3F`, `p4F`,
and `p4T`.
:::
:::

::: {.function-item}
::: {.function-call}
`classical_features(path)`
:::
::: {.function-body}
Returns classical line-profile summaries for comparison: total line flux,
equivalent width, peak flux, FWHM, `W80`, centroid, line dispersion, skewness,
and excess kurtosis.
:::
:::

::: {.function-item}
::: {.function-call}
`as_spectral_path(u, F)`
:::
::: {.function-body}
Builds the two-column matrix expected by the feature functions, with the
ordered coordinate in the first column and the measured signal in the second.
:::
:::

:::

## Paper notation

::: {.notation-table}
::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p2`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Signed area in the ordered-coordinate--flux plane.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p3u`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Third-order coordinate sensitive to where profile imbalance occurs along the
ordered coordinate.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p3F`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Third-order coordinate sensitive to whether structure is associated with
brighter or fainter parts of the line.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p4F`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Fourth-order flux modulation.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p4T`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Fourth-order mixed coordinate used for bends, shoulders, and multi-component
profile structure.
:::
:::

::: {.notation-row}
::: {.notation-symbol}
`p_pm`
:::
::: {.notation-meaning}
Signed ordering of emission and absorption in mixed-sign profiles.
:::
:::
:::
